title: EEOC and Opexus

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: EEOC and Opexus
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is narrowed request relative to my first request about the same matter. Narrowed Request: For dates January 1, 2022 to February 28, 2026, I request all correspondence between EEOC and Opexus management and staff - including staff and management from AINS, which is the former company name of Opexus.

Background:
On March 3, 2026, I responded to EEOC's February 26, 2026 acknowledgment notice (or as stated by EEOC to be on February 27, 2026) and stated: "Regarding # 820-2026-005851, I thank you for the response. I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA Office. As a standing request, ask that EEOC not require me to log into a potentially compromised FOIA system to view any updates , etc. This is especially salient because of recent serious security concerns.... Thank you for considering my requests and desire for secure and transparent communications." Again I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA portal and I elect not to use it for reasons I have explained. I request that all communications occur outside of the EEOC FOIA platform and be transparently sent for public view.

On April 9, 2026, EEOC Office of Legal Counsel sent a letter stating among other things, "This letter is to inform you that we would like to have a conversation with you in hopes of clarifying and narrowing your request. We conducted a search on February 27, 2026, based on your request as written, which resulted in over 124,000 items. We do not have an exact page count, but assuming that doing an industry standard of about three (3)pages per item, the actual page count is going to be likely around the neighborhood of over 372,000 pages. We are putting this request on hold so that we may have a conversation with you concerning how you can effectively limit the scope of this request, so that it can be processed. " Unfortunately EEOC only provided me with 10 days to respond. I was on annual leave during these 10 days. Thus, I am resubmitting this modified request.

On May 10, 2026, I resubmitted a narrow request resulting in a May 28, 2026 rejection. Please consider my reasonable accommodation request of at least 20 days to respond to any notices through the MuckRock portal. Also, I ask that in the future, that you not close any of my requests if I do not respond in less than 20 days. This is a standing request. Phone calls and access to the EEOC FOIA portal are not options for me. 

I will always remain interested in receiving all requests until they are received.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jessica Elm

Thank you for your interest in public records of Boston Police Department. Your request has been received and is being processed. Your request was received in this office on 6/7/2026 10:19:18 PM and given the reference number B001846-060726 for tracking purposes. 
If this is a media request, please contact the Office of Media Relations directly at 617.343.4520. 
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records: 

Any surveillance footage, video recordings, photographs, or other visual records depicting the Emerson College Palestine Encampment Clearing, Boylston Place alleyway, night of 4/24/24, early morning 4/25/24: 

This includes footage captured by: 
Boston Police Department cameras; 
Traffic or public safety cameras operated by or accessible to the Boston Police Department; 
Mobile surveillance units; 
Aerial surveillance platforms; and 
Any other recording systems operated by or on behalf of the Boston Police Department. 
Any logs, indexes, inventories, retention schedules, chain-of-custody records, or metadata identifying the existence, storage, retention, transfer, or destruction of such footage. 

Any records reflecting the sharing, dissemination, transfer, or provision of footage, photographs, intelligence, reports, or other information concerning the Emerson College Palestine Encampment Clearing to: 
The Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI); 
The Department of Homeland Security; 
The Massachusetts State Police; 
The Boston Regional Intelligence Center (BRIC); 
or any other federal, state, or local law enforcement agency. 

Any emails, memoranda, reports, requests, intelligence products, bulletins, or communications concerning: 
Surveillance of the Emerson College Palestine Encampment Clearing; 
Collection of information regarding participants in the the Emerson College Palestine Encampment; 
Requests for surveillance footage related to the the Emerson College Palestine Encampment; or 
Sharing of surveillance footage or intelligence with federal agencies. 
Any records sufficient to identify whether surveillance footage of the protest was retained, archived, deleted, or otherwise disposed of, and the date on which any such action occurred. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage. 

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Tatum Schutt

title: Flock Safety Organization and Network Logs | Q2 2026 | American Fork Police Department

communications.subject: Government Records Access and Management Act Request: Flock Safety Organization and Network Logs | Q2 2026 | American Fork Police Department
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.

The report should include data logged from the period of 1/1/2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.

The report should include data logged from the period of 1/1/2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing.

3a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data;

3b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data;

3c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information;

3d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

a

title: SWIM historical data

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I request records from January 1, 2021 through the date of search sufficient to identify, describe, and locate existing Bureau of Transportation Statistics records, datasets, repositories, tables, files, archives, data lake/lakehouse tables, object-store locations, or other electronic records repositories that store historical FAA System Wide Information Management (SWIM) data in source-faithful or technically normalized form.

This request applies to records maintained by BTS, OST-R, the Volpe National Transportation Systems Center, contractors, cloud service providers, or other entities to the extent those records are maintained for BTS or are otherwise agency records subject to FOIA.

For purposes of this request, “source-faithful or technically normalized SWIM data” means SWIM messages, payloads, records, files, or tables that preserve the substantive informational content of FAA SWIM messages substantially as published by or received from FAA SWIM services.

This includes repositories where SWIM messages have been technically normalized, flattened, parsed, mapped, or converted for storage or retrieval, including conversion from XML, JSON, or other SWIM message formats into Parquet, Avro, CSV, database tables, Delta Lake tables, partitioned files, or other machine-readable storage formats.

A repository is within the scope of this request even if some transport-layer, envelope, header, routing, broker, or ingestion metadata was not retained, provided that the repository preserves the substantive SWIM payload fields or message content in a source-faithful form.

This request includes repositories where SWIM data has been minimally processed for technical storage or retrieval, including serialization, deserialization, compression, decompression, schema validation, parsing, flattening, field mapping, type conversion, timestamp normalization, deduplication, indexing, partitioning, topic tagging, source-service tagging, or conversion to columnar or tabular formats.

This request includes only the following categories of records:

1. dataset inventories, table lists, file inventories, object-store inventories, metadata catalog entries, data catalog records, or other records identifying BTS, OST-R, Volpe, contractor-operated, or cloud-hosted repositories containing source-faithful or technically normalized historical FAA SWIM data;

2. schemas, data dictionaries, field definitions, table definitions, topic lists, service lists, message-type lists, XML schemas, source-to-target mappings, transformation mappings, and records identifying native storage formats for those repositories;

3. records identifying the source SWIM service, feed, topic, message type, producer system, ingestion timestamp, message timestamp, source message identifier if retained, partition key, storage location, retention period, or export method for those repositories;

4. retention schedules, archive policies, deletion policies, records-management documentation, and data lifecycle documentation for those repositories;

5. architecture diagrams, ingestion diagrams, pipeline diagrams, interface control documents, data flow diagrams, memoranda of understanding, data-sharing agreements, and system design documents showing FAA SWIM data being ingested into BTS, OST-R, Volpe, contractor-operated, cloud-hosted, data lake, lakehouse, database, or analytics environments, but only to the extent those records identify or describe source-faithful or technically normalized SWIM repositories;

6. existing export procedures, query procedures, APIs, standard operating procedures, scripts, jobs, notebooks, or documentation used to retrieve historical records from those repositories.

This request includes source-faithful or technically normalized repositories associated with FAA SWIM services or feeds including SFDPS, En Route Flight Data Publication or Query, En Route Airspace Data Publication or Query, En Route General Message Publication or Query, En Route Operational Data Publication or Query, STDDS, TFMS/TFMData, TBFM, TFDM, ITWS, FNS/NOTAM, and other FAA SWIM services, to the extent such records are maintained by or for BTS.

This request does not seek downstream analytical, curated, modeled, fused, enriched, aggregated, scored, machine-learning, reporting, dashboard, business-intelligence, performance-measurement, delay-attribution, commercial-flight-database output, public-statistics, or decision-support datasets merely because they use SWIM as an input.

The distinction intended by this request is between source-faithful technical normalizations, which are included, and downstream analytical derivations, which are excluded.

Please do not process downstream derived products unless they are necessary to identify the existence, location, schema, retention period, format, or exportability of source-faithful or technically normalized SWIM repositories.

I am not requesting live operational access, credentials, passwords, private keys, internal network information, or operational security procedures. I am not asking DOT, BTS, OST-R, Volpe, or any contractor to create a new analytical product, write a new computer program, or perform analysis. I seek existing records, metadata, schemas, documentation, inventories, and export/query procedures maintained in the ordinary course.

If this request is considered broad, please process first records identifying any BTS, OST-R, Volpe, contractor-operated, or cloud-hosted landing-zone, staging, bronze-layer, source table, message archive, query archive, replay store, object-store path, Parquet table, Delta Lake table, or equivalent repository that stores FAA SWIM data before downstream analytical transformation.

Please produce responsive records in native electronic format where readily reproducible, including associated metadata. If native format is not readily reproducible, please produce records in a machine-readable format such as CSV, JSON, XML, Parquet, or PDF for documentation records.

Please process this request on a rolling basis. As responsive records are located, reviewed, and cleared for release, please produce them in interim batches rather than waiting until all potentially responsive records have been processed.

If any portion is withheld, please release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions and identify the exemption asserted for each withholding. If DOT/BTS believes any portion of this request is unclear or overbroad, please contact me before denying or closing the request so that I may narrow it.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jonathan Rudenberg

For purposes of this request, “raw or source-faithful SWIM data” means SWIM messages, payloads, records, or tables that preserve the substantive informational content of FAA SWIM messages substantially as published by or received from FAA SWIM services.

This includes repositories where SWIM messages have been technically normalized, flattened, parsed, mapped, or converted for storage or retrieval, including conversion from XML, JSON, or other message formats into Parquet, Avro, CSV, database tables, Delta Lake tables, partitioned files, or other machine-readable storage formats.

Arizona State University ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-277 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for submitting a public records request to Arizona State University. We will begin reviewing your request now. If we need additional information to process it, or if we are unable to do so, we will be in touch with you shortly.  Otherwise, we will begin gathering any responsive records from the relevant departments and/or individuals at ASU. After the records have been gathered and individually reviewed to determine if they are responsive and whether any confidential information needs to be withheld or redacted, they will be provided to you. The amount of time necessary to complete this process varies depending on a variety of factors, including how extensive the request is, how responsive information is stored, and what kinds of review and redaction are necessary.  Please be aware that ASU receives a large number of records requests and we may be occupied in processing requests received prior to yours. You are welcome to check on the status of your request at any time through the online portal you used to submit your request (https://asu.nextrequest.com/). When the responsive documents are ready for you, you may also use this portal to access them and to pay any charges associated with your request.
